user_text:
The process at Harro Höfliger Verpackungsmaschinen GmbH begins with the generation of the software and document draft by the Production / Design and Development team.

Next, an overview of the machine is obtained.

Then, several tasks are carried out in parallel: the SPS project is completed by station, the HMI project is completed in stages, and the safety project is completed.

Once all programs are ready, the software is tested.

Afterward, a software design review is conducted.

Finally, the software is ready for commissioning.
output_text:
The process begins with the initiation of the project. The first step involves generating a draft of the software and its accompanying documentation. Once this draft is prepared, the next step is to obtain an overview of the machine that will be involved in the project.

After acquiring the machine overview, a decision point is reached where one of three different paths can be taken. The first option is to complete the SPS project by station. Alternatively, the second option is to complete the HMI project in stages. The third option is to complete the safety project. Only one of these paths is chosen and completed at a time.

Once any of these projects—SPS, HMI, or safety—is completed, the process converges back to a single path. The next step is to test the software to ensure it functions correctly. Following successful testing, a software design review is conducted to evaluate the design and make any necessary improvements.

After the design review, the software is deemed ready for commissioning, indicating that it is prepared for deployment and use. The process concludes with this final step, marking the end of the project.
